title: aconitum ranunculifolium subsp. platanifolium
intro: Aconitum ranunculifolium subsp. platanifolium, also known as the broad-leaved monk's hood, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the Aconitum genus. It is native to certain regions and is known for its toxic properties due to the presence of aconitine, a powerful neurotoxin. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including pain, inflammation, and fever, although its use is often cautioned due to its toxicity. Aconitum ranunculifolium subsp. platanifolium is typically prepared as a tincture, decoction, or topical cream, but its use should only be under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
